merger_name: INPEX – minority interests in Beetaloo development projects

targets.name: Daly Waters FSDA South Pty Ltd
Daly Waters FSDA North Pty Ltd

merger_description:
INPEX Corporation, through its wholly owned Australian subsidiaries - INPEX FSDA N&S Pty Ltd and INPEX BCD Pty Ltd (collectively, **INPEX**) entered into a farm-in agreement with Daly Waters Energy LP (**DWE**) to acquire:

1. a 20% interest in the Beetaloo Central Development project;
2. an 11.25% interest in the First Strategic Development Area (**FSDA**) South project; and
3. an 11.25% interest in the FSDA North project, (together, the **Acquisition**).

The projects will be operated as an unincorporated joint venture between DWE (via its subsidiaries), Tamboran Resources Corporation (via its subsidiaries) and INPEX (acting via its subsidiaries) (together, the **Joint Venture Parties**), and will be governed by separate joint operating agreements.

Pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Retention Licences that will be obtained for each project and a joint operating agreement, the Joint Venture Parties will explore for, and undertake appraisal activities to confirm, a commercial resource of conventional and unconventional oil and gas resources within each project area.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
INPEX Corporation (INPEX), via wholly owned subsidiaries INPEX
FSDA N&S Pty Ltd and INPEX BCD Pty Ltd, applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:
• a 20% interest in the Beetaloo Central Development project
• an 11.25% interest in the First Strategic Development Area
South project, and
• an 11.25% interest in the FSDA North project
(together, the Projects)
via a farm-in agreement with Daly Waters Energy LP (DWE), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

The acquirer, INPEX, is an oil and gas exploration and production
company headquartered in Tokyo, Japan. Its main business includes
research, exploration, development, production and sales of oil,
natural gas and other mineral resources, as well as investment and
lending to the companies engaged in these activities.
In Australia, INPEX:
• owns a majority stake and operates the Icthys LNG project in
offshore Western Australia
• has a minority participating interest in Prelude FLNG Project
in offshore Western Australia
• has a minority participating interest in Darwin LNG Project,
located in the Northern Territory
• has a minority participating interest in the Ravensworth Oil
Field in offshore Western Australia
• has a 50% interest in Potentia Energy.
Furthermore, INPEX is engaged in oil and gas exploration through
interests in leases in and around Western Australia and the Northern
Territory, principally in the area surrounding the Ichthys LNG Project.
INPEX also supplies LNG in the global seaborn LNG industry and
natural gas in the Northern Territory.
The Projects, which are the target of the Acquisition, are located
within the Beetaloo Sub-basin, an undeveloped but prospective shale
gas region in the Northern Territory.
The Projects would be operated as an unincorporated joint venture
between DWE (via its subsidiaries), Tamboran Resources
Corporation (via its subsidiaries) and INPEX (acting via its
subsidiaries) (together, the Joint Venture Parties), and would be
governed by separate joint operating agreements. The acquirer,
INPEX, would acquire a minority interest in each of the Projects and
would not be a project operator for any of the Projects.
Pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Retention Licences that
will be obtained for each Project and a joint operating agreement, the
Joint Venture Parties will explore for, and undertake appraisal
activities to confirm a commercial resource of conventional and
unconventional oil and gas resources within each Project area.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular
a. the Acquisition would lead to a very small increase in
INPEX’s share of gas or LNG production in Australia (to the
extent gas or LNG is produced from the Projects)
b. there are alternative producers and suppliers of natural gas
or LNG in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
